title: Capturing Structural Locality in Non-parametric Language Models

structured.summary: The paper explores integrating structural locality into non-parametric language models to improve their performance on Java source code and Wikipedia text. By augmenting retrieval processes with hierarchical features, the approach aims to refine model efficacy beyond traditional contextual similarity metrics.

strengths.evidence: Experiments demonstrate noticeable performance gains on Java source code and Wikipedia text by incorporating locality features.
The method uses only 5 parameters for Java and 7 for Wikipedia, showing its efficiency.

weaknesses.evidence: The approach shares similarities with domain adaptation techniques, which is acknowledged in Section 3 of the paper.
Manual definition of hierarchical features raises questions about scalability and applicability to other datasets, as discussed in Section 3.
The evaluation does not compare against state-of-the-art baselines, limiting the context of reported improvements.
The integration of hierarchical attributes into distance metrics is not detailed enough, as discussed in Section 3.
Details on hyperparameters or code availability are missing, hindering reproduction of the results outlined in Sections 2 and 3.

questions.question: How are the specific parameters for Java and Wikipedia determined?
questions.motivation: Understanding the rationale behind these choices can validate the effectiveness and scalability of the approach.

critique_points.point_001: Limited novelty.. The approach shares similarities with domain adaptation techniques, which is acknowledged in Section 3 of the paper
critique_points.point_002: Generalizability concerns.. Manual definition of hierarchical features raises questions about scalability and applicability to other datasets, as discussed in Section 3
critique_points.point_003: Lack of competitive benchmarks.. The evaluation does not compare against state-of-the-art baselines, limiting the context of reported improvements
critique_points.point_004: Insufficient clarity on methodology.. The integration of hierarchical attributes into distance metrics is not detailed enough, as discussed in Section 3
critique_points.point_005: Reproducibility issues.. Details on hyperparameters or code availability are missing, hindering reproduction of the results outlined in Sections 2 and 3
